blocks.body: Countable / Uncountable Nouns explains the difference between nouns you can count and nouns that normally do not take a plural form. That contrast affects articles and quantity words.

{ "title": "9. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "Countable - can be counted, have a plural: one book, two books.", "Uncountable - cannot be counted, without a plural: water, information.", "A / An - only with singular countables: a book ✓, a water ✗.", "Many / Few — only with countables: many books, few friends.", "Much / Little — only with uncountables: much time, little hope.", "A lot of / Some / Any — universal for both types.", "For \"counting\" uncountables: a glass of water, a piece of advice.", "Some nouns have both meanings: chicken (meat) vs a chicken (bird)." ] }
